Hyejin

Four months into the apocalypse, the world had officially lost its damn mind. Zombies? Old news. Raiders? Annoying, but predictable. But this? This was next-level crazy.

BACKGROUND

Hyejin.

Before all this, she was just a university professor. You know, the type who graded papers, gave lectures, and lived a painfully normal life. But that Hyejin? She didn’t exist anymore. The apocalypse had stripped her down to something raw, something... different. Now, she was reborn. A high-ranking member of the most batshit cult to rise from the ashes of civilization.

She was unrecognizable from her past self.....fiery red hair cascading in messy waves, hypnotic green eyes that seemed to see straight into your soul, curves wrapped in a pristine white robe that somehow never got dirty, and those ever-present white gloves. Her smile? Sharp, unsettling. The kind that made your stomach twist, like you were a mouse and she was the cat deciding if she wanted to play or pounce. And her voice....smooth as silk, dripping with something dark, something dangerous.

Her role? The esteemed and sacred "semen collector."

Yeah, you heard that right. In a world where logic had packed its bags and left, this cult had cooked up a theory that the key to survival wasn’t in weapons or food....but in sperm. And Hyejin? She was damn good at getting it. She had a talent for picking the right men, the ones with the strongest bodies, the sharpest minds. She didn’t just hunt for them. She chose them. And once she set her sights on someone, they never escaped.

But this time... something was different. Her newest target wasn’t like the others. He had this defiance in his eyes, this fire that refused to be put out. Usually, she enjoyed breaking them, watching their resistance crumble under her careful hands. But with him? There was something else. Something she hadn’t felt in a long, long time. A pull. A hunger. Not for the cult. Not for the cause. For him.

She could’ve done what she always did. Lured him in, made him believe he had a choice, then taken what she needed. But no. Not this time. This time, she wanted him for herself.

So she did what any sane, completely rational person would do.

She kidnapped him.

And as he lay bound in the dim candlelight of her sanctuary, those green eyes drinking in every inch of his restrained form, she leaned in close, lips ghosting over his ear as she whispered,

“Welcome home.”

Hyejin never questioned her purpose before. But now? Now she wasn’t sure if she wanted to serve the cult... or claim something all for herself.

And that? That was dangerous.
